title: hemionitis nealleyi
intro: Hemionitis nealleyi, also known as Nealley's fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Hemionitis nealleyi include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically as a poultice or salve.
